summary: Backend testing complete for Channel Broadcaster API. All 32 pytest tests PASSED (100%). Covered: health, auth (register/login/me/otp/forgot-reset), wallet (get/recharge/transactions), channels (insufficient balance 402, create, list, get, monitor, renew ~60 days after 2nd cycle, disable/enable, delete), dashboard, notifications (list + mark-all-read), admin (overview/users/channels/wallet adjust/disable/delete/expire-now), and role guard (403 for non-admins). MOCK OTP, MOCK reset token, and MOCK Razorpay recharge all working as designed. Flussonic client failed gracefully (as expected) and channel creation still succeeded returning publish/playback URLs. No blocking issues found.

minor.issue: Endpoint returns mock_otp even for non-existent emails (this is intentional to prevent enumeration, per code comment). Note: for non-existent users the returned OTP is not persisted, so subsequent verify will 400 with 'Invalid OTP' — behaviour is safe but worth documenting.
No upper bound / rate limit on recharge amount. Real Razorpay integration will add this later; acceptable for MOCK.
Response body does not include a transaction object like /wallet/recharge does. Consider returning the created transaction for admin UI parity.

action_items: No blocking fixes required. Backend is production-ready for the tested scope.
Optional: return the created transaction object from POST /api/admin/wallet/adjust for consistency with /wallet/recharge.
Optional: add a sanity max cap on /wallet/recharge amount for now while Razorpay is mocked.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py at ~735 lines is at the acceptable limit; consider splitting into routers/ (auth, wallet, channels, admin) if it grows further.
Env keys are read with os.environ[...] for critical keys — good (fails fast). Non-critical keys use os.environ.get with defaults — acceptable.

clean_doc() correctly strips _id, password_hash, otp_hash, reset_token. _serialize_channel() also removes _id and serializes datetimes to iso. No MongoDB ObjectId leak observed in any tested response.
Password hashing via bcrypt with gensalt() — correct.
JWT includes exp/iat and role — good. Verification handles ExpiredSignatureError and InvalidTokenError distinctly.

Wallet debit for channel creation is not refunded if Flussonic provisioning fails — this matches the stated business rule but should be surfaced to the user in the UI (flussonic_provisioned flag is returned).
expire_channels_task() runs hourly via lifespan background asyncio task — good. Admin has manual /admin/expire-now endpoint (works).

CORS allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec and browsers may reject credentialed requests. For a JWT bearer-token flow this is not a real issue (no cookies), but consider tightening.
No rate limiting on /auth/login, /auth/otp/request, /auth/forgot-password — brute force risk. Add slowapi or similar before production.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates one fresh TEST_user_<uuid>@example.com and up to 2 TEST channels. All test data prefixed 'TEST' for easy cleanup. No production data touched. Admin user is seeded by backend on startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API is fully green. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_broadcaster_api.py is idempotent (unique email per run) and runs in ~5s. Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env for base URL (NOT EXPO_BACKEND_URL — that key does not exist here). Admin credentials: admin@broadcaster.com / Admin@1234. Channel creation debits 500 unconditionally (no refund on Flussonic failure by design). Flussonic is unreachable from testbed but channel creation still returns 200 with flussonic_provisioned=false — verified working.
